name: @orrgal1/forge
description: Self-contained forge chain for a PR — no dependency on other plugins. /forge-setup maps host-repo tooling (build/test/lint/typecheck/codegen/devenv/localenv) into a gitignored .forge/ directory; every other skill resolves repo commands through it, so forge adopts into any repo with zero coupling. /forge-goals captures the PR's intended end-state as a goal list. /forge-scenarios drafts when:/then: scenarios covering each goal (component-tier-observable behavior only). /forge-tests writes a test per scenario (or attaches an existing test), preferring the component tier. /forge-design maps every scenario to the design elements (components, symbols, data flow) that will satisfy it. /forge-audit aggregates the full attestation chain: PR has goals loyal to its source → each goal covered by ≥1 scenario → each scenario attached to a real component-tier test → each test body matches its scenario when/then + AAA → linked tests pass. Per-layer skills (/forge-verify-goals, /forge-verify-scenarios, /forge-verify-tests, /forge-verify-match, /forge-verify-runs) expose each layer for step-by-step attestation. /forge-impl-green drives linked tests to green. /forge-audit-green drives the audit to PASS via a fix-loop. /forge-ci-green drives PR CI to green. /forge-review-green drives the aggregated multi-channel review to 0 blockers + 0 majors. /forge-review (opt-in) is the forge-chain-aware multi-channel PR review: fans out review channels in parallel (lens-fanout by default; opt-in code-review-builtin and security-review-builtin wrappers; custom channels via .forge/review-channels/) and aggregates findings to a single ranked verdict. /forge-start opens the chain: resolves a source into a 1-3 sentence brief, lands a sentinel commit, pushes, opens a draft PR. /forge runs the whole arc end-to-end: start (draft PR + minimal brief) → goals (AWAIT_GOALS_REVIEW) → design (AWAIT_DESIGN_REVIEW) → scenarios → tests → impl → audit-green → ci-green → review-green → final ci → READY. Two modes: auto (pause only at goals + design), manual (pause after every phase). Operator resumes via /forge approve and /forge iterate "<feedback>". Artifacts live under .pr-artifacts/<branch-slug>/forge/. Pre-flight aid: /forge-map builds structured JSON snapshots of host-repo domains (db schema via /forge-map-db, HTTP routes via /forge-map-api, pub/sub topics via /forge-map-events, env-var surface via /forge-map-config) under $FORGE_HOME/maps/main/ (ground truth) with lazy branch-divergent forks under maps/branches/<branch>/ that absorb back to main on merge; ad-hoc operator-described maps via /forge-map adhoc <name> --prompt "<description>". /forge-tool packages ad-hoc flows discovered during a session (seed test db, rotate secrets, backfill safely) into reusable tools under $FORGE_HOME/tools/<name>, registered in $FORGE_HOME/forge.toml [tools.<name>]: script / instructions / dir / agent forms; captured via interactive Q&A, --from-session inspection, or --from-prompt paste; first-class lookup so other forge skills can resolve a tool by name. All repo state (forge.toml, commands, tools, review, review-channels, lenses, personas, maps) lives at $FORGE_HOME — default ~/.claude/forge/<repo-key>/ — so worktrees of the same repo share state; legacy <repo>/.forge/ honored during the migration window. /forge-setup --migrate user moves repos to the new layout.
